_doc: The high-leverage HKDF-driven sub-dimension of dim #08. The accent skeleton fixes the four strong-beat scale-degrees of the phrase (out of the chord-tone set, weighted by harmonic function); a separate micro_shape interpolates the 3 weak-beat-positions between each pair of adjacent strong beats. micro_shape values are normalized contour offsets in the [-1,+1] range, applied additively to the contour sample at each weak slot. 8 entries indexed by the single byte from HKDF label `melody/accent_skeleton`. Distinguishability per byte is the highest in the melody pipeline (see C4 in findings).

spec.strong_beat_chord_tones: Decoder takes the 4 chord-tone slots from the current chord stack at strong beats (downbeat of bars 1-4 of the phrase, or beats 1/3 within a 4/4 bar — interpretation set by the form's harmonic_rhythm). For each strong beat, a 2-bit sub-field of the byte (or HKDF top-up if more chord tones exist) selects from {root, 3, 5, 7-or-extension} weighted [0.35, 0.30, 0.25, 0.10].
spec.weak_beat_interp: Between successive strong beats, micro_shape supplies 3 normalized offsets that are added to the contour-derived sample at each weak slot. The sum of the strong-beat target plus the offset is then resolved via the active scale_subset.
spec.ordering_rule: If the phrase has fewer than 4 strong beats (e.g. 3/4, 7/8), only the first N slots of the skeleton are consumed; remaining bytes are ignored deterministically (high-order bits first per dim #12 ordering).
spec.anti_collision: If the chosen strong-beat tone would create a parallel-fifth/octave with the bass at chord onset (per C7 fix), the resolver substitutes the next-weighted chord tone from the same byte sub-field.
